items.title: China cuts gasoline price for 1st time this year despite Iran war
In bellicose Memorial Day speeches, leaders vow to keep fighting; Netanyahu heckled
items.description: Defense minister says Hezbollah leader will 'pay with his head'; protester interrupts PM when he says he brought back all the hostages from Gaza; Gantz warns of 'deceptive calm' The post In bellicose Memorial Day speeches, leaders vow to keep fighting; Netanyahu heckled appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: As the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline looms, here are the main sticking points
items.description: The status of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear enrichment program are among the biggest obstacles to extending the truce, which expires Wednesday evening.

items.title: Iran-US war: Four scenarios for what’s next as talks stumble
items.description: Between an expiring ceasefire and a possible deal lies a range of ways in which the war could proceed, analysts say.

items.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 21 April: Talks unclear; Hormuz tensions continue
items.description: Uncertainty surrounds possible US-Iran talks in Pakistan, with no confirmation yet that they will go ahead. At the same time, tensions persist in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over maritime security, while the humanitarian crisis in Lebanon continues despite the ceasefire. Stay with us for live updates. App us…

items.title: Macron to meet Lebanon's PM in Paris to 'reaffirm commitment' for Israel truce
items.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam is set to meet with French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace late on Tuesday, with the president expected to reaffirm his support for the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, three days after the death of a French peacekeeper in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
items.description: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

items.title: Rachel Ehrenfeld: Why Targeting Iran's 'Shadow Economy' Hits The IRGC Where It Hurts
items.description: With a cease-fire in the US-Israeli war on Iran due to expire on April 22, US actions at sea are drawing renewed attention to a central issue: How deeply Iran’s military establishment is embedded in the country’s economy.

items.title: Amid ceasefire in Lebanon, a rush home – if it’s reachable and still there
items.description: As civilians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon try to return to their war-damaged homes, a new Israeli occupation zone in the country’s south poses an obstacle for many. Meanwhile, the ceasefire has reduced but not eliminated the fighting.

items.title: Trump, altamente improbabile l'estensione del cessate il fuoco con l'Iran
Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC
items.description: Uganda's military has said that it and forces from the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo freed some 200 hostages in eastern DRC in a joint operation against Islamist militants known as the ADF.

items.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
items.description: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.

items.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
items.description: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rightsMyanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists.The south-east Asian country…

items.title: One Dead in Kherson Drone Strike and Three Wounded in Zaporizhzhia Bombing
items.description: Russian forces targeted a civilian car in Kherson and dropped guided aerial bombs on the village of Balabyne, continuing a wave of deadly assaults across southern Ukraine.

items.title: Zelensky Says Oil Sanctions Relief Provides Billions for Russian Military
items.description: "Every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war," Zelensky said.
